title: helenium edwardsianum
intro: Helenium edwardsianum, also known as desert marigold or orange sunflower, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Helenium edwardsianum have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
